raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "A medical school will provide jobs, improve healthcare access, and create opportunities for aspiring physicians and nurses in Kern County.",
    "The San Joaquin Valley is among California’s most medically underserved areas with a lack of adequate physician population per capita.",
    "In 2021, the California Health Care Foundation reported that the San Joaquin Valley had just 47 primary care physicians per 100,000 residents.",
    "The recommended supply ratio of primary care physicians by the US Department of Health and Human Services is 60-80 per 100,000 residents.",
    "The state average of primary care physicians in California is 60 per 100,000 residents.",
    "The San Joaquin Valley is one of the fastest-growing, poorest, and least-healthy regions of California.",
    "The eight counties in the San Joaquin Valley experience some of the poorest air quality and highest rates of poverty and uninsured individuals in the nation.",
    "41% of the 4 million residents in the San Joaquin Valley are covered by Medi-Cal, including 70% of children in Kern County.",
    "Valley counties consistently rank at the bottom for health outcomes when compared to other parts of California.",
    "America is currently experiencing a shortage of primary care physicians, with over 83 million Americans living in areas with insufficient access to them."
  ]
}
```
